divergence_notes: Large-n hypothesis: both hosted theorems (plby, jayyhk) witness 'large n' with an explicit `10 <= Fintype.card V`; the draft instead uses the asymptotic form `forall-eventually n in atTop` (FC house style for 'sufficiently large', cf. Erdos369). The literal all-n reading of the problem text is false (Keevash-Sudakov: threshold is 10 > 6^2/4 at n=6, binom(n,2) for n<=5), so the eventual form is the faithful solved reading; it is weaker than the hosted `n >= 10` version in that it does not pin the explicit threshold.

Not-in-monochromatic-triangle predicate: hosted theorems define `NIMT` / `SimpleGraph.nimt` (a named def plus an AFrame structure) and count `#(SimpleGraph.nimt C).edgeFinset`; FC has no such def, so the draft inlines the predicate as a set of non-diagonal `Sym2 (Fin n)` edges (quantified over all representations `e = s(x, y)` for symmetry) and counts with `Set.ncard`. Same extension: an edge {x,y} is in a monochromatic triangle iff some third vertex z has C(xz) = C(yz) = C(xy).

Vertex type: hosted theorems quantify over an abstract `{V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]` with `Fintype.card V`; the draft uses concrete `Fin n` (house style for counting statements, cf. Erdos1104/Erdos920), which is equivalent up to relabelling since the statement only depends on card.
Bound: `n ^ 2 / 4` is natural (floor) division, identical to the hosted `(Fintype.card V) ^ 2 / 4`; for an integer count this is equivalent to the real bound n^2/4 in the problem text.

No formal_proof link: link_allowed=false (no signed unconditional vsa_), so the draft carries no `formal_proof` attribute even though upstream state is proved (Lean); hosted URLs remain in inputs.md only.
